title: WhatsApp Integration
summary: WhatsApp integration via the Baileys unofficial bridge, covering QR code pairing, bot vs self-chat mode, session persistence, voice message transcription, and markdown conversion for WhatsApp formatting.
key_concepts: Baileys bridge: unofficial WhatsApp Web protocol
QR code pairing on first run

env_vars.name: WHATSAPP_SESSION_PATH
env_vars.description: Directory to persist WhatsApp session credentials

env_vars.name: WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_CONTACTS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated E.164 phone numbers allowed to interact with the bot

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway; on first run prints QR code to pair WhatsApp
Delete saved session credentials to force re-pairing

config_keys.description: E.164 phone numbers allowed to chat
Enable self-chat mode (message yourself to interact with Hermes)
Transcribe incoming voice messages via STT (default: true)

code_examples.title: Self-chat mode (message yourself)
code_examples.code:
whatsapp:
  enabled: true
  self_chat: true  # interact by messaging your own WhatsApp number

code_examples.title: First run QR pairing
code_examples.code:
hermes gateway
# Prints QR code to terminal
# Scan with WhatsApp > Linked Devices > Link a Device
# Session saved to session_path for future restarts

code_examples.title: Reset session to re-pair
code_examples.code:
hermes gateway whatsapp reset-session
# Deletes saved credentials, next gateway start will show QR again

gotchas: Baileys uses the unofficial WhatsApp Web protocol; WhatsApp may block accounts that appear automated
Session credentials expire if not used for 14+ days; reset-session and re-pair required
WhatsApp does not support multiple simultaneous sessions on the same phone number
Groups are disabled by default to avoid unintended responses; enable explicitly with groups: true
WhatsApp formatting uses *bold*, _italic_, ~strikethrough~,  — Hermes converts standard Markdown to this format
Voice messages are received as .ogg Opus files; transcription requires a configured STT provider
Rate limiting: WhatsApp may throttle or ban accounts sending too many messages per hour
full_text:
## WhatsApp

Connect Hermes to WhatsApp via the [Baileys](https://github.com/WhiskeySockets/Baileys) bridge (unofficial WhatsApp Web protocol).

> **Warning:** Baileys uses WhatsApp's unofficial web protocol. Use with a dedicated account; there is a small risk of account suspension for automated messaging.

### Setup

Enable WhatsApp in your config:

```yaml
whatsapp:
  enabled: true
  session_path: /data/whatsapp-session
  allowed_contacts:
    - "+15551234567"
```

Start the gateway:

```bash
hermes gateway
```

On first run, a QR code prints to the terminal. Open WhatsApp on your phone → **Linked Devices → Link a Device** → scan the QR code. Hermes saves the session to  so future restarts skip the QR step.

### Self-chat mode

If you want to use your own number (not a separate bot account), enable self-chat:

```yaml
whatsapp:
  self_chat: true
```

Message yourself in WhatsApp to interact with Hermes.

### Session persistence

Session credentials are saved as JSON files in . Back up this directory. If the session becomes invalid (not used for 14+ days, or WhatsApp logs it out), reset and re-pair:

```bash
hermes gateway whatsapp reset-session
```

### Group chats

Groups are disabled by default. Enable:

```yaml
whatsapp:
  groups: true
```

In groups, Hermes only responds when explicitly mentioned ().

### Voice messages

Incoming voice messages (.ogg Opus) are automatically transcribed if  (default) and a STT provider is configured. The transcription is passed to the LLM as the message text.

### Formatting

Hermes converts standard Markdown to WhatsApp's formatting syntax:
-  → 
-  → 
-  → 
-    →   
- block → block (same)

### Allowlist

Restrict to specific phone numbers in E.164 format:

```yaml
whatsapp:
  allowed_contacts:
    - "+15551234567"
    - "+447700900123"
```

Leave empty to allow all contacts (not recommended for public bots).
